title: Reduce operator ecosystem redundancy
goal: Reduce duplicated scheduling, execution residue, status projections, maps, event capture and proposal-only work while preserving the distinct authority of Bureau, Grabowski, RepoBrief/Lenskit, GitHub, CI and runtime.
completion: Bureau scheduler lanes have explicit unique inputs, outputs, order and consumers, with redundant lanes consolidated or justified.
Grabowski worktree and transient-unit residue is classified, reconciled and safely archived or retained with evidence.
Steuerboard has one bounded role or a reviewed migration/retirement path.

Cabinet is reduced to an app-independent Heimgewebe system catalog with one authority matrix, one registry, one generated view, no duplicated operational status and a reviewed migration path away from the external Cabinet runtime.
Heimlern and Vibe-Lab work requires a named consumer, decision target, success metric and expiry.
Chronik stores only operator events that add value beyond Git, Bureau receipts and source logs.
Remote and local runtime consumers plus view usage are measured before any final shutdown decision.
RepoBrief/Lenskit daily consumption uses a bounded artifact surface with redundant formats diagnostic-only.
WGX retains only genuinely shared fleet checks and does not duplicate Grabowski execution or Bureau coordination.
Leitstand is the single general operator display and duplicate generic panels are removed or source-linked.

migration_order: inventory and export local app data
decouple and retire external app runtime
